home.tagline: Software Engineer, Data Scientist, and AI Engineer.
home.bio: I'm Jacob, a software engineer and data scientist student based in Arizona. I specialize in building and deploying machine learning models, and web applications.

about.heading: I'm Jacobo Ganon a software engineer and data scientist.
about.paragraph1Before: This page will not contain much information on my accomplishments as it is mostly for information about me. If you are looking for my previous projects visit the
about.paragraph2: I started programming when I was 15 years old with a friend of mine. When we started out it was just a way to challenge ourselves and we quickly progressed. We started with simple projects that could be done in a couple of days and then moved on to more complex projects.
about.paragraph3: We quickly ran into issues because in Mexico software development is not as popular as it is in the United States so we had to learn everything on our own. However, this also gave us a great opportunity to work with company owners and develop software for them, we both quickly went into app development where we discovered how difficult scalability can be.
about.paragraph4: To continue down this path I decided to study in the United States. I am currently a student in Arizona State University where I have learnt how to build efficient and scalable projects. With this knowledge I have been able to build projects in Mexico that has helped companies grow and make their processes more efficient and hope to do so in United States soon as well.

articles.intro: Big projects that I have worked on collected in chronological order.

article_caliza.title: Caliza Gym Full Stack Web Development
article_caliza.description: Caliza is a multi-tenant SaaS platform for gym and fitness center management. What started as custom software for a single gymnastics and bouldering gym in Mexico has grown into a franchise-ready system that handles class scheduling, memberships, employee payroll, financial analytics, and QR-based attendance across multiple gym locations.
article_caliza.intro: Caliza is a gymnastics and bouldering gym located in Mexico. There is little software to help manage gyms in Mexico, and the software that does exist is often outdated and difficult to use. What began as a custom solution built with the gym owners has evolved into a fully multi-tenant SaaS platform now used across multiple gym locations. The platform handles everything the gym needs to operate: class scheduling, member management, employee payroll, inventory, finances, and attendance tracking via QR codes. It has since been expanded to support franchise networks, allowing multiple gyms to be managed under a single umbrella with shared analytics.
article_caliza.multiTenantBody: The platform is built with a database-driven multi-tenant architecture. Each gym gets its own subdomain (e.g., calizagym.com) and the system resolves all gym-specific configuration from the database on every request, including pricing, Stripe account, email settings, and subscription status. There is no code-level separation between tenants; isolation is enforced entirely at the data layer. A 7-day grace period allows gyms to continue operating after a subscription expires while renewal is being processed.
article_caliza.franchiseBody: Gym owners can manage multiple locations under a single franchise account. A dedicated analytics dashboard aggregates revenue, membership counts, and attendance across all locations, giving franchise administrators a unified view of their entire operation.
article_caliza.feature_0: Members can browse and register for classes, change class slots as long as spots are available, and track their progression through training programs.
article_caliza.feature_1: Payments are handled online via Stripe, supporting both one-time payments and subscription models. The system automatically updates member status when payments are processed or expire.
article_caliza.feature_2: QR codes are used for gym entry and class attendance, tracking both member and employee check-ins in real time.
article_caliza.feature_3: Employees have a dedicated section to view their schedule, classes they are teaching, and the members they are responsible for. Payroll is calculated automatically from their hours and rates.
article_caliza.feature_4: Financial reports consolidate all transactions into a unified dashboard with exportable breakdowns, covering online payments, in-gym sales, and product purchases.
article_caliza.feature_5: An announcement system lets administrators broadcast messages to targeted member segments (e.g., members with expiring memberships or upcoming birthdays).
article_caliza.feature_6: Scheduled emails are sent automatically for birthdays, membership expirations, and achievement milestones using QStash cron jobs.
article_caliza.feature_7: A product catalog allows gyms to sell merchandise, with inventory tracking and purchase history tied to each member's account.
article_caliza.feature_8: AI-powered features are integrated for select workflows using Google Generative AI and OpenAI.
article_caliza.tech_0: Framework: Next.js 16 (T3 Stack, App Router), TypeScript 5.8+, Turbo monorepo, pnpm
article_caliza.tech_1: Frontend: React 19, Tailwind CSS v4, shadcn/ui, TanStack Query
article_caliza.tech_2: Backend: tRPC v11 (type-safe RPC), Prisma ORM, PostgreSQL
article_caliza.tech_3: Authentication: Better Auth 1.4.0 with Google OAuth and magic links via Resend
article_caliza.tech_4: Payments: Stripe and Stripe Connect (franchise revenue splits)
article_caliza.tech_5: Email: Resend with per-gym sender configuration and React Email templates
article_caliza.tech_6: Scheduled Tasks: QStash for cron jobs (payment processing, birthday and expiration emails)
article_caliza.tech_7: File Uploads: UploadThing backed by Supabase Storage
article_caliza.tech_8: AI: Google Generative AI, OpenAI via Vercel AI SDK
article_caliza.demoBody: Although the full administration side is not publicly accessible, the member-facing side can be seen at calizagym.com. For more information contact me at 22jacganon@gmail.com to set up a meeting and see the full platform in action.

article_hdo.title: Hacienda Del Oro Software Engineer
article_hdo.description: HDO (Hacienda del Oro) is a country club in Mexico that focuses on equitation. The owner has multiple companies and needed a way to facilitate the communication and manage HDO. I worked with the owner to help him solve the problems.
article_hdo.intro: HDO (Hacienda del Oro) is a country club in Mexico that focuses on equitation. The owner has multiple companies and needed a way to facilitate the communication and manage HDO. I worked with the owner to help him solve the problems.
article_hdo.communicationBody: HDO needs to communicate with the owner's other companies to manage the finances and the employees. They previously used email to communicate but now all the information was available to everyone in real time. There was an authorization system that allowed the owner to create new roles and give different permissions to each company or employee.
article_hdo.feature_0: HDO employees could upload receipts and a parser would extract the information and save it in the database.
article_hdo.feature_1: The finances department could see the expenses as soon as they were uploaded.
article_hdo.feature_2: Employee management with the days worked calculating the salary and printing out the payroll sheets.
article_hdo.feature_3: The owner could see the finances and administration of HDO.
article_hdo.conclusionBody: This demo is not available as it was created for a specific company and is not open to the public. However, this project taught me a lot about how to design software for a company. The demo for the gym software contains many of the same features with improvements and can be found at Caliza.
article_hdo.authBody: To create a new role the owner would give it a name and then select the permissions that the role would have. The users could apply for a role and it could be seen at the owner's dashboard. In this same place the owner could provide roles to employees without a request.

article_moga.title: App Development for Moga
article_moga.description: Moga is a gymnastics gym where I first found the need for a gym management software in Mexico. However, the owners were only looking for a management software for classes and product inventory and this is the problem I solved with this application.
article_moga.intro: Moga is a gymnastics gym where I first found the need for a gym management software in Mexico. However, the owners were only looking for a management software for classes and product inventory and this is the problem I solved with this application.
article_moga.classesBody: This application was based on local storage and was only available on the owner's computer. It helped manage the class schedule, and the gym's inventory. The clients had to be added manually and register the information that could be used for emergencies. When a student had a class and their time was close to expiration the owner and client would get a notification. For the inventory the owner could add products and see the quantity of each product. When a product was sold the owner could update the quantity and see the total sales for the day.
article_moga.feature_0: Classes: The owner could add, delete, and update classes.
article_moga.feature_1: Inventory: The owner could add, delete, and update products.
article_moga.feature_2: Clients: The owner could add, delete, update clients, and add time to them.
article_moga.feature_3: Notifications: The owner and client would get a notification when a class was close to expiration.
article_moga.feature_4: Expiration Portal: The owner could see the clients that had classes close to expiration in an easy to use portal.
article_moga.feature_5: Format Data: Format the data and download in an Excel file.
article_moga.conclusionBody: This project was a great way to start my career in software development, and look for the needs in the market. To get a demo of this project contact 22jacganon@gmail.com to setup a meeting and see the software. A better version of this software is available at Caliza, as it is a software I developed later to fully adapt to the needs of the clients instead of only what they ask for.

article_evotion.title: Evotion: AI Platform Full Stack Development
article_evotion.description: Evotion is an AI chat platform that brings together 30+ of the best language models in one place. Built with a pay-per-use and subscription model, it lets users access ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok, and many more through a single unified interface with an innovative fusion mode that automatically selects and combines models for optimal results.
article_evotion.intro: Evotion is an AI chat platform that brings together 30+ of the best language models in one place. The core idea is simple: instead of juggling multiple subscriptions and switching between tabs, users get access to ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok, and many more through a single unified interface at the best price.
article_evotion.fusionBody: The most distinctive feature of Evotion is its Fusion Mode, a system that automatically selects the best model for each query and, when beneficial, runs multiple models in parallel and synthesizes their outputs into a single refined response. Instead of needing to know which model excels at code, which is best for creative writing, and which handles reasoning tasks best, Fusion Mode handles that decision automatically. The result is consistently higher-quality responses without requiring the user to manage model selection manually.
article_evotion.feature_0: Access to 30+ language models (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok, Mistral, and more) through a single interface.
article_evotion.feature_1: Fusion Mode that automatically selects and combines models based on the type of query.
article_evotion.feature_2: Multi-modal support: send text, images, and documents (PDFs) in the same conversation.
article_evotion.feature_3: Real-time usage tracking with precise cost calculations per message and per session.
article_evotion.feature_4: Web search integration that grounds responses in up-to-date information.
article_evotion.feature_5: Artifact generation for documents, code, and structured outputs.
article_evotion.feature_6: Subscription and pay-per-use billing managed through Stripe.
article_evotion.feature_7: Device fingerprinting, MFA support, rate limiting, and audit logging for security.
article_evotion.tech_1: Backend: tRPC, Next.js API routes, PostgreSQL, Drizzle ORM
article_evotion.tech_2: AI Integration: Vercel AI SDK with adapters for all major model providers
article_evotion.tech_3: Authentication: NextAuth.js with multiple OAuth providers and MFA
article_evotion.tech_5: Real-time Streaming: Server-Sent Events for live model response streaming
article_evotion.tech_6: Monorepo: Turborepo for managing the frontend and backend packages
article_evotion.conclusionBody: Evotion is live and available at evotion.ai. For a walkthrough of the platform or to discuss the architecture in more detail, contact me at 22jacganon@gmail.com.
article_evotion.image2Alt: Evotion model selection and fusion mode

article_vinos.title: Vinos: Restaurant Wine Management Platform
article_vinos.description: Vinos is a wine management platform for restaurants and wine providers. It tracks inventory at the individual bottle level, comes with a tablet point-of-sale app for floor staff, supports multiple restaurant locations with role-based access, and handles purchase orders, supplier management, and sales analytics.
article_vinos.intro: Vinos is a wine management platform built for restaurants that want real control over their cellar. Most restaurant wine systems treat wine as generic inventory, which falls apart once you need to track individual bottles, partial pours, and where everything is physically stored. Vinos handles all of that, so the team always knows what has been sold, what is left, and where each bottle sits in the cellar.
article_vinos.architectureBody: Each restaurant gets its own isolated inventory, employees, and sales history. A seven-tier role system covers everyone from general staff up through wine cellar workers, managers, inspectors, and administrators, so each person only sees and does what their role allows. Owners with multiple locations can manage all of them from one account, with each location running independently. Wine providers are their own entity type in the system. Restaurants source wine from registered providers, who go through an admin approval process before their catalog shows up. That keeps supplier relationships organized and easy to audit.
article_vinos.posBody: Floor staff use dedicated tablets to log bottle sales, glass pours, and tastings as they happen. Each tablet is registered as a trusted device using a public key, and every request it sends is signed with HMAC-SHA256 and validated on the server with nonce checks to stop replay attacks. Staff sign in on the tablet with a short numeric code, which is fast enough for a busy service. Every transaction is tied to the person who made it, and orders are grouped by session and synced back to the main platform so managers can review everything.
article_vinos.feature_0: Individual bottle tracking with status and remaining volume, so a partially-poured bottle is always accounted for.
article_vinos.feature_1: Wine profiles with taste ratings (sweetness, acidity, tannin, body on a 1–5 scale), flavor tags, winery, vintage, and pricing per bottle and per glass.
article_vinos.feature_2: Cellar location management: bottles are assigned to physical storage areas and can be transferred between locations with a full movement log.
article_vinos.feature_3: Minimum stock thresholds with alerts so managers know when to reorder before a wine runs out.
article_vinos.feature_4: Purchase order workflow: create orders to suppliers, track delivery status, and confirm batch arrivals to update inventory automatically.
article_vinos.feature_5: Analytics dashboard with 30-day revenue rankings by wine, sales trends over time, and breakdowns by sale type and employee.
article_vinos.feature_6: Full transaction history covering every inventory event: entries, sales, glass pours, tastings, transfers, removals, and returns.
article_vinos.feature_7: Stripe integration for subscription billing across restaurant accounts.
article_vinos.tech_0: Framework: Next.js 16 (App Router), TypeScript, pnpm monorepo with Turbo
article_vinos.tech_1: Frontend: React 19, Tailwind CSS v4, shadcn/ui, TanStack Query
article_vinos.tech_3: Authentication: NextAuth v5 with Google OAuth and email verification
article_vinos.tech_4: Tablet POS: Expo (React Native) with HMAC-SHA256 signed requests and device enrollment
article_vinos.tech_7: Task Queue: Upstash QStash for background jobs
article_vinos.demoBody: Vinos is not publicly accessible as the platform is deployed for private restaurant use. For a full walkthrough of the platform or to discuss the architecture, contact me at 22jacganon@gmail.com.
article_vinos.image1Alt: Vinos restaurant dashboard and wine inventory overview
article_vinos.image2Alt: Vinos purchase orders and supplier delivery tracking
article_vinos.image3Alt: Vinos wine cellar inventory management and storage locations
